name: AURA by skinlab. – Cold Plasma Device
shortDescription: AURA by skinlab. is a cold atmospheric plasma (CAP) technology cosmetic device that provides antibacterial effects, enhanced active ingredient absorption, and skin regeneration – gently and painlessly.
description:
AURA by skinlab. is a modern, professional cold atmospheric plasma (Cold Atmospheric Plasma – CAP) device that creates ionized air at low temperature.

CAP technology, upon contact with the skin, does not burn but triggers beneficial biological effects: it creates an antibacterial environment, enhances active ingredient absorption, and supports the skin's natural regeneration.

AURA is the ideal choice for estheticians seeking effective, safe, and affordable cold plasma technology.
metaTitle: AURA Cold Plasma Device | CAP Technology | SkinLab Hungary
metaDescription: AURA by skinlab. cold plasma device with CAP technology. Antibacterial effect, active ingredient delivery, skin regeneration. Esthetician competence, CE certified.

videoDescription.headline: What is CAP – and why introduce it now?
paragraphs: Cold Atmospheric Plasma (CAP) technology creates ionized air at low temperature. Upon contact with the skin, it doesn't burn but triggers beneficial biological effects: it reduces bacteria, enhances active ingredient absorption, and supports skin regeneration.
AURA is an affordable, professional CAP device that enables you to offer cold plasma treatments in your salon – at competitive pricing with proven technology.
Plasma treatments are increasingly sought after by clients, and with AURA you can enter this rapidly growing market segment.

featureRows.headline: Why do professionals choose AURA?
featureRows.subtitle: CAP technology, antibacterial effect, active ingredient delivery – in one device

features.title: What is Cold Atmospheric Plasma (CAP)?
features.description:
Plasma is the fourth state of matter. CAP technology creates ionized plasma from normal air at room temperature, which does not damage the skin.

Biochemical effects of plasma:

Reactive oxygen and nitrogen species are generated
Loosens intercellular junctions
Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory
Stimulates cell renewal and skin regeneration

features.title: Enhanced active ingredient absorption
features.description:
Plasma reduces the tightness of intercellular junctions (e.g., E-cadherin), allowing serums and ampoules to penetrate deeper into the skin.
More effective when combined with mesotherapy serums
Deeper penetration of skin renewal ingredients
Ideal preparation before hyaluronic acid treatments

features.title: Antibacterial effect – acne and inflamed skin
features.description:
Plasma produces reactive oxygen and nitrogen molecules that naturally reduce bacteria and disinfect the skin.
Acne skin treatment
Reducing inflammatory skin problems
Wound healing support
non-invasivepainlessno recovery time

features.title: Pigmentation fading and skin barrier strengthening
features.description:
Plasma can help reduce melanin activity and improve the absorption of brightening ingredients, while also stimulating cell renewal and skin regeneration.
Fading freckles and pigmentation spots
Sun-damaged skin treatment
Sensitive skin and damaged skin barrier support
Soothing irritated skin

features.title: Esthetician competence – safe and legal use
features.description:
The AURA cold plasma device is designed for cosmetic aesthetic use and falls entirely within the esthetician competence scope.
Not a medical device
Can be used in cosmetic settings
CE certified
Legal documentation provided

features.title: Who is AURA recommended for?
features.description:
Estheticians wanting to introduce cold plasma technology
Salons seeking an affordable CAP solution
For acne treatment and problem skin therapy
To complement active ingredient delivery treatments
For skin regeneration and skin barrier strengthening protocols

training.headline: Operator training – official adult education program
training.intro: We provide professional operator training for the AURA cold plasma device within the framework of officially registered adult education. The goal of the training is for participants to fully understand the operation and applications of CAP technology.
items: Operating principles of Cold Atmospheric Plasma (CAP)
Antibacterial treatment protocols for acne skin
Active ingredient delivery techniques with serums
closingParagraphs: You are welcome to bring a model to the training so you can learn the treatment techniques in a real-world environment.
Upon completion of the training, we issue an official certificate confirming your competence in the professional operation of the AURA device.
If you're interested in the training opportunity, please contact us through any of our channels to arrange an appointment!

testimonials.name: Fehér Nikolett
testimonials.text: AURA was my first cold plasma device, and it's a <mark>perfect entry-level tool</mark>. My acne clients achieve fantastic results with it, and the active ingredient delivery function is also excellent. Great value for money!

testimonials.name: Balogh Eszter
testimonials.text: I specifically chose AURA because <mark>it offers CAP technology at an affordable price</mark>. My clients love that the treatment is completely painless, and the effectiveness of active ingredient treatments has truly improved. It paid for itself in two months!

expertQuote.quote: AURA is an excellent choice for estheticians who want to enter the cold plasma market without immediately buying the most expensive device. CAP technology has proven effectiveness, and AURA makes it accessible at a professional level.
expertQuote.author: Horváth László

faq.question: What's the difference between AURA and THE FROST?
faq.answer: AURA is a Cold Atmospheric Plasma (CAP) device primarily focused on antibacterial effect, active ingredient delivery, and skin regeneration. THE FROST is a more complex system with 9 functions, including cold plasma, thermoplasma, and ozone plasma technologies. AURA is an excellent entry-level device, while THE FROST is the complete plasma treatment system.

faq.question: Can I legally use AURA as an esthetician?
faq.answer: Yes! AURA is designed for cosmetic aesthetic purposes and falls entirely within the esthetician competence scope. It comes with CE certification and legal documentation.

faq.question: What treatments is it suitable for?
faq.answer: AURA can be used for acne treatment support, bacteria reduction, pigmentation fading, enhancing active ingredient absorption, stimulating skin regeneration, and soothing inflamed skin.

faq.question: Is the treatment painful?
faq.answer: No, CAP technology is completely painless. The plasma operates at room temperature, doesn't burn, and doesn't damage the skin. The treatment is pleasant, and no anesthesia is needed.

faq.question: How quickly does AURA pay for itself?
faq.answer: Cold plasma treatment prices typically range from 8,000–18,000 Ft. With 3–4 clients per day, the device generally pays for itself within 2–3 months. Use our ROI calculator for exact figures!

faq.question: Are consumables needed?
faq.answer: Plasma treatments themselves don't require consumables – the device generates plasma from normal air. If you're performing active ingredient delivery treatments, you'll naturally need the appropriate serum or ampoule.

faq.question: What training comes with the device?
faq.answer: A one-day operator training within an official adult education framework, which is free with device purchase. The training covers CAP technology operation, treatment protocols, and includes practical treatments. We issue a certificate.
